subject: Geography
title: Sea Caves
description: A fast breakdown of Sea Caves for builders.

hook.text: Sea caves are carved by waves smashing against cliffs thousands of times per day.

buildup.text: Waves exploit cracks in rock. Salt, sand, and compressed air widen the gaps over centuries.

discovery.text: The Blue Grotto in Capri glows electric blue as sunlight refracts through underwater openings.

twist.text: Some sea caves collapse inward, creating blowholes that shoot water 30 meters into the air.

climax.text: The ocean never stops hammering. Given enough time, it will hollow out any coastline.

punchline.text: The sea builds caves with brute force, not patience.

quiz.question: What creates sea caves?
options: Waves repeatedly smashing against cliff weaknesses
Rivers flowing underground to the coast
Volcanic activity beneath the ocean floor
